picks.title: Moana/Viana (live action)
picks.blurb: Live-action adaptation of the animated adventure with Moana sailing beyond the reef.
picks.why: Family-friendly ocean adventure with beloved characters

picks.title: Finding Emily
picks.blurb: Musician dials wrong number but searches for the mysterious girl he can't forget.
picks.why: Charming romantic comedy with campus chaos

picks.title: Carolina Carolina
picks.blurb: Woman escapes small-town life with charming con artist on chaotic Southern roadtrip.
picks.why: Modern Bonnie and Clyde with romance and danger

picks.title: Concerts by Candlelight
picks.blurb: Unique candlelit concerts with tributes to artists like Adele, Coldplay, and Bridgerton soundtrack with hundreds of candles.

picks.title: Hortus Summer Tours & Evening hours
picks.blurb: Guided botanical tour past hidden gems and monumental greenhouses; garden open late; relax on terrace with drinks and live music.

picks.title: Larissa Sansour: Rogue Agents of History
picks.blurb: Contemporary art exhibition exploring history and identity through Sansour's provocative visual works.

picks.title: World Press Photo
picks.blurb: Prestigious annual exhibition of world's best photojournalism and documentary photography.
picks.why: Iconic global exhibition, spanning this weekend
picks.source: I amsterdam · Your Little Black Book

picks.title: The Invite
picks.venue: Pathé Bioscopen, Kriterion, Studio-K, LAB111, Rialto, FC Hye
picks.blurb: Dinner party with neighbors spirals into chaos as hidden secrets and desires surface.
picks.why: Dark comedy revealing life behind closed doors

picks.title: La Petite Dernière
picks.venue: Pathé Bioscopen, Studio-K, FC Hyena, Ketelhuis, The Movies,
picks.blurb: French-Algerian teenager discovers freedom and first love while studying in Paris.
picks.why: Coming-of-age drama between tradition and new life

picks.title: Ekō – Japan in twee beeldverhalen
picks.blurb: Japanese maritime exhibition exploring cultural and historical connections through two visual narratives.

picks.title: Nederland – Japan. Door de tijd verbonden
picks.blurb: Explore Japan-Netherlands cultural exchange through centuries via rare objects, screens, swords, and lacquer boxes.
picks.why: Unique Japan treasures at the Royal Palace

picks.title: Martine Gutierrez – Wunderkind
picks.blurb: Personal exploration of identity and self-expression through photos, self-portraits, childhood toys, and handmade objects.
picks.why: Intimate journey into artistic identity and self-discovery

picks.title: Liefde op de Grachten – 30 Jaar Canal Parade
picks.blurb: Exhibition celebrating Canal Parade's 30-year history through photos, art, and personal stories of Amsterdam's iconic Pride event.
picks.why: Canal Parade history with photos and personal stories

picks.title: The Little Big Things
picks.blurb: West End hit musical based on true story about how one moment transforms life, featuring live music and emotion.

picks.title: Julidans international dance festival
picks.blurb: Cutting-edge contemporary dance: Saturday's Winged blends South Indian with modern choreography at Meervaart.

picks.title: ARTIS Summer Evenings
picks.blurb: Zoo after sunset with live emerging musical talent in bandstand; stroll among animals at golden hour with birdsong and greenery.

picks.title: Summer lunchconcerts on the Duyschot organ
picks.blurb:
A musical lunch break
 Admission is free, as are the seats; the Westerkerk would be very grateful for your contribution to the collection afterwards.
 Evan Boge

picks.title: 10SERIES - M-High, Lasse Top, Shanne

picks.blurb: baesmith, Fouk, Giu Nunez, Nikko Axzul and more at TBA.

picks.title: Tom Trago All Night Long
picks.blurb: Tom Trago at Het Sieraad.

picks.title: Tropical Nomads Amsterdam presents: Avan7
picks.blurb: Club night at Toekomstmuziek.

picks.title: Unai Trotti • Hannecart • Tommy Chikara
picks.blurb: Unai Trotti, Hannecart, Tommy Chikara at Radio Radio.

picks.blurb: Rishi Romero, Oscar Osorio at Club Leidse.

picks.blurb: Vie Aimee, Esi (De Re) at Skatecafe.

picks.title: THE JUNCTION
picks.blurb: Travis Allen at Yellow House.

picks.blurb: Club night at Panama.

picks.blurb: Roman Flügel, Shubostar, Field Notes DJs at Studio 508.

picks.blurb: angelboy, De Ambassade, job sifre, justin case and more at murmur.

picks.title: Wifey
picks.blurb: Club night at Chin Chin Club.

picks.title: Pink
picks.blurb: Club night at Supperclub.

picks.title: Eline, Lylo, Portente
picks.blurb: Eline, LYLO (NL), Portente at BRET.

picks.title: PROJECT w/ Boss Priester
picks.blurb: Club night at nachbar.

picks.title: KNALL with Alex O'Rion
picks.blurb: Alex O'Rion, GALAGO (BE) at Houtbaar Haarlem.

picks.title: Kinetic Energy
picks.blurb: Gimmeamfbreakbeat, Gysèle at OT301.

picks.title: co-curation by Oceanic: Kiernan Laveaux, Oceanic, LazerGazer
picks.blurb: Kiernan Laveaux, Oceanic, LazerGazer at Garage Noord.

picks.title: Restroom hosted by Maat Sound
picks.blurb: Club night at Shelter Amsterdam.

picks.title: Tomato Summer Food & Wine Tasting with Corkin Wijn
picks.blurb: Italian wine tasting with four wines paired with tomato-based bites by chef Alexandra from Ali e Radici.

picks.title: Île de Bisous
picks.blurb: Oyster bistro Bisous stages a two-day Côte d'Azur fantasy on the water — champagne, small plates and live music.

picks.title: Eye(s) Open: New Perspectives on Colonial Film Heritage
picks.blurb: Eleven artists respond to Eye's colonial-era film collection from Indonesia and Suriname with ten new commissioned works exploring archival reinterpretation.
picks.why: Major exhibition on film history and artistic response

picks.title: Queer Power
picks.blurb: Eye celebrates World Pride with films, talks and events showcasing queer makers and creativity from around the world through September.
picks.why: World Pride programming, diverse queer cinema

picks.title: Omoka pop-up at Woodies on NDSM boat
picks.blurb: African cuisine pop-up on a ship at NDSM with authentic dishes, cocktails, music and IJwater views.

picks.title: Strand van Oost
picks.blurb: A beach on a roof in Oost: sand-and-sunset bar on top of Orchid House that makes the city briefly disappear.
picks.source: Ness canon · Your Little Black Book

picks.title: Le Petit Bouillon d'Amsterdam
picks.blurb: The little Zuid sibling of the Paris-style bouillon: classic French bistro plates and small bites on a sunny terrace, still absurdly affordable.
picks.source: Your Little Black Book · Ness canon

picks.title: Kwaku Summer Festival
picks.blurb: Multicultural summer festival with music, food, and culture running through August. Don't miss this vibrant celebration.
picks.source: Your Little Black Book · I amsterdam

picks.title: Nara Nara
picks.blurb: Egyptian chef's new restaurant on Dapperplein with Cairo flavors, shared dishes and warm communal dining.

picks.title: Café Publiek
picks.blurb: New neighborhood all-day café on Dapperplein with warm vintage interior, large terrace and good vibes.
picks.why: Welcoming local café with great terrace

picks.title: Cinair Europe
picks.blurb: Open-air cinema at historic buildings and hidden gardens with silent cinema headphones.
picks.why: Next-level outdoor film experience in unique locations

picks.title: The Jollof Club
picks.blurb: West African hotspot with modern take on Ghanaian, Nigerian and Senegalese cuisine, wine and storytelling.

picks.title: Pllek — waterfront hang
picks.blurb: A cargo-container café-bar on the NDSM wharf — big windows onto the IJ, sofas, and a wood stove when it turns.
picks.why: Indoor seating · IJ views · cozy when grey

picks.title: Café Chris — oldest brown café
picks.blurb: Open since 1624: a creaky, golden-lit Jordaan brown café for a slow afternoon beer while it pours outside.

picks.title: Foodhallen
picks.blurb: An indoor food hall in a converted tram depot — 20-odd stalls, long tables and two bars under one roof.
picks.why: Indoor · grazing for a crowd · weather-proof

picks.title: Milkshake Festival
picks.blurb: Open-minded, gloriously dressed-up dance festival mixing house, disco, pop, techno and dancehall with drag and voguing — kicking off Pride week.

picks.title: Sauna Deco — Art Deco baths
picks.blurb: A 1920s Art Deco bathhouse (salvaged from a Paris department store) with saunas, steam and a quiet tea room.
picks.why: Warm · restorative · the ultimate cold-and-wet antidote

picks.title: Vondelpark playgrounds
picks.blurb: Amsterdam’s favourite park: open-air playgrounds, the Kinderkookkafé, ducks and room to run.
picks.why: Free · easy any time with kids

picks.title: De Blauwe Parade
picks.blurb: The 1887 Delft-blue bar is back as a cocktail kroeg under top bartender Milos Stanisic — local spirits, ossenworst, weekly-changing list.

picks.title: Mokumboot: Sloepje huren
picks.blurb: Rent a small boat to explore Amsterdam's canals and waterways. Perfect summer activity combining adventure, nature, and city discovery.
picks.why: Unique canal experience, outdoor summer fun

picks.title: Kinderfilms in Eye
picks.blurb: Family film screenings with free scavenger hunts and colouring activities. Great rainy-day option with serious cinema credentials.
picks.why: Free activities, reputable cinema, curated for kids

picks.title: Zomervakantie Joods Museum
picks.blurb: Holiday baking and crafting workshops at the Jewish Museum. Hands-on creative activities designed for summer-holiday kids.

picks.title: The Movies — arthouse cinema
picks.blurb: The Netherlands’ oldest cinema still running — Art Deco rooms, a dinner-and-a-film deal, arthouse bills.
picks.why: Indoor · dinner + a film · perfect when it’s wet

picks.title: Vermut
picks.blurb: A dedicated vermouth-and-aperitivo bar on the JP Heijestraat — Spanish-style early evenings, no reservations culture required.
picks.why: Aperitivo hour finally gets a home

picks.title: Bar Copa
picks.blurb: From the Bar Parry and Balthazar's Keuken people: a tiny weekend-only natural wine bar — wine on tap, Mallorcan charcuterie.

picks.title: Two Story
picks.venue: Two Story — gallery, design store & café
picks.blurb: An 18th-century canal house rebuilt by Copenhagen's Atelier Axo: design store up front, courtyard café, gallery rotating every three months.
picks.why: Copenhagen design eye, Amsterdam canal house

picks.title: East Beach — rooftop city beach
picks.blurb: A new 800m² rooftop city beach above Oosterpark — sand, beach chairs, drinks, open to all.

picks.title: Marocaine
picks.blurb: Chef Rachid El Mouhandiz, who scored a 9- in Het Parool, turns his Jordaan spot into a full ode to Moroccan cooking.

picks.title: De Pimpelmees
picks.blurb: Cosy neighbourhood wine bar — European wines from €6, Italian snacks, free chips. Opened early 2026.

picks.title: De Poldervogel
picks.blurb: Alumni of De Kas and Vuurtoreneiland open a seasonal neighborhood restaurant — refined comfort food, sharing plates, zero fuss.

picks.title: Het Landjuweel
picks.blurb: Amsterdam's oldest festival (since 1975) turns the free-haven artist village of Ruigoord into four days of DJs, live music, rituals and art performances.

picks.title: Foam
picks.blurb: A nimble photography museum in a canal house — big-name retrospectives and emerging talent, always worth the drop-in.

picks.title: Droog
picks.blurb: Where Dutch conceptual design was born: gallery, shop, courtyard garden and a single hotel room in a Staalstraat warehouse.

picks.title: MENDO
picks.blurb: A self-described candy store for book aficionados: black-walled cabinet of photography, design and fashion monographs.

picks.title: Moise
picks.blurb: An understated De Pijp boutique with a razor-sharp eye — Acne, Lemaire, Marant and the like, hand-picked and beautifully shown.

picks.title: Patta
picks.blurb: The homegrown streetwear label and culture hub — drops, sneakers and the community that put Amsterdam on the map.

picks.title: X Bank
picks.blurb: A concept store in a former bank showing 180+ Dutch fashion, design and art labels — couture to hand-thrown ceramics.
picks.why: Best of Dutch design · one room

picks.title: Melkweg
picks.blurb: A former dairy turned multi-room culture machine — live music, club nights, film and a gallery, all under one roof.

picks.title: Mobilia
picks.blurb: Three floors of high design on the Utrechtsestraat — icons by the masters alongside more attainable covetables.

picks.title: Utrecht
picks.blurb: Amsterdam’s quieter little sister — wharf-side canals, the soaring Dom Tower and a real café culture, minus the crowds.

picks.title: Vlieger
picks.blurb: A two-floor temple to paper since 1869 — hundreds of colours, handmade sheets and card stacked to the ceiling.

picks.title: Efteling
picks.blurb: Europe’s oldest fairytale theme park — enchanted forest, dark rides and proper coasters in a storybook world.

picks.title: IJ-Hallen
picks.blurb: Europe’s biggest flea market — 750 stalls across two NDSM shipyard warehouses, a proper treasure-hunt of vintage and junk.

picks.title: Micropia
picks.blurb: The world’s only microbe museum — microscopes, a kiss-o-meter and the tiny life all around us.

picks.title: Paradiso
picks.blurb: Amsterdam’s legendary music hall in a converted church — stained glass overhead, a heaving floor below, indie to techno.

picks.title: Kriterion
picks.blurb: A legendary student-run cinema and café since 1945 — arthouse programming with a buzzy terrace.

picks.title: Madurodam
picks.blurb: The whole of the Netherlands at 1:25 — miniature canals, Schiphol and windmills kids can tower over.

picks.title: Nxt Museum
picks.blurb: Warehouse-scale new-media museum in Noord: room-sized audiovisual installations rewired each season by artists and coders.

picks.title: Muiderslot
picks.blurb: A moated medieval castle on the IJmeer — turrets, falconry and a walled garden, the best-preserved fortress in the country.

picks.title: Dappermarkt
picks.blurb: A buzzing, multicultural Oost street market — spices, textiles and world food at honest neighbourhood prices.

picks.title: Garage Noord
picks.blurb: A club, kitchen and cultural space in a former garage — daytime plates, late-night dancing.
picks.why: Kitchen by day · club by night

picks.title: Rijksmuseum
picks.blurb: The Netherlands’ treasure house — Rembrandt’s Night Watch, Vermeer and the Golden Age masters in a cathedral of a building.
picks.why: The Night Watch · the big one

picks.title: Bloemenmarkt
picks.blurb: The world’s only floating flower market — barges of tulips, bulbs and seeds moored along the Singel since 1862.

picks.title: De Filmhallen
picks.blurb: A nine-screen arthouse cinema in a converted tram depot — with a bustling food hall right next door.
picks.why: Arthouse · in the old tram depot

picks.title: Eye Filmmuseum — repertory screening
picks.blurb: Across the IJ by free ferry: a striking film museum with a daily repertory programme and a river-view café.
picks.why: Indoor · a film + the ferry ride · weather-proof

picks.title: Huis Marseille
picks.blurb: The city’s first photography museum, across two canal houses with a hidden garden — beautifully curated, never crowded.

picks.title: Museum Van Loon
picks.blurb: A patrician canal house still furnished as lived: portraits, coach house, and the quietest formal garden inside the ring.

picks.title: Sexyland World
picks.blurb: A members’ club owned by 1,000 people — a different host and a different vibe every single night.

picks.title: Wils at the Farm
picks.blurb: Joris Bijdendijk and Thomas Val cook open-fire dinners at the farm that grows their ingredients — long tables, meadow views.
picks.why: Star kitchen relocated to its farm

picks.title: Marken & Volendam
picks.blurb: Two former Zuiderzee fishing villages — wooden houses, working harbours and smoked eel, an easy half-day from Centraal.

picks.title: Zandvoort Beach
picks.blurb: The nearest North Sea beach — wide sands, beach clubs and a direct train from Centraal; in summer half the city decamps here.

picks.title: Albert Cuypmarkt
picks.blurb: The Netherlands’ biggest street market — 250-odd stalls of stroopwafels, cheese, fabric and flowers the length of De Pijp.

picks.title: Dekmantel Festival
picks.blurb: Peerless underground electronic festival in the forest — the sharpest techno, house and experimental line-up of the year.

picks.title: Royal Concertgebouw
picks.blurb: One of the world’s great concert halls — peerless acoustics, a resident world-class orchestra, and free Wednesday lunch concerts.

picks.title: Waterlooplein Flea Market
picks.blurb: The city’s oldest flea market — secondhand everything, vintage denim, records and bric-a-brac spread out beside the Stopera.

picks.title: Cinema Culinair
picks.blurb: Watch food films like Chef while eating the exact dishes featured on screen.
picks.why: Sensory dining experience paired with cinema

picks.title: BRET: Rooléh (Sat) / Mau P + Joëlla Jackson (Sun)
picks.blurb: Container venue with lush green park. Intimate electronic music space with dining and dancing.
picks.why: Transformed warehouse space, dual weekend lineup

picks.title: American Myth Memory – David Levinthal Photographs
picks.blurb: Photographic exploration of American mythology and collective memory through acclaimed photographer David Levinthal's distinctive visual perspective.
picks.why: Contemporary photography exhibition on American culture

picks.title: Stoornis of my life
picks.blurb: Alex Klaasen stars in this hilarious, touching musical about autism, family, and life's unexpected turns with live music.

picks.title: De Hortus Botanicus summer evenings
picks.blurb: Botanical garden open late with live music, golden hour strolls and sunny terrace drinks until August.
picks.why: Perfect summer evening in blooming gardens

picks.title: BAK
picks.blurb: A light-filled warehouse with an open kitchen and an art-covered wall; a set seasonal menu of produce-driven dishes.

picks.title: ARTIS Royal Zoo
picks.blurb: The country’s oldest zoo — big cats, an aquarium and a planetarium across a leafy 19th-century park.
picks.why: All-day with kids · indoor + outdoor mix
